learning_disability: My sister had a speech impediment as a child that required speech services for 3 years
head: produces
combination_example2: Mommy go over there right now
Combination Example 2: Mommy go over there right now

services: Miles was in Early Intervention services through REACH (provider in Western Mass) and then through Riverside (provider in Somerville, MA). After undergoing an assessment at 18 months, he didn't meet the clinical criteria for enrollment, but the clinicians used "clinical judgement" to make him eligible for services. The issues seem to be in expressive vocabulary and language production, while language comprehension and other aspects of cognitive, social, and motor development are on track. His expressive vocabulary was deemed at the low end of the average range. The clinicians think that the expressive language delays are due to his inability to make the muscles in his face, tongue, and mouth create the sounds, and also because he's super busy and doesn't stick around long enough to stare at people's faces when they're making words. Miles was just reassessed after 6 months of services, and he no longer qualifies for services and has transitioned out of EI.
